findings.hypothesis: User-controlled HTTP input flows into File/Path constructors enabling path traversal

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Static file handler path traversal
 * @description Path traversal in static file serving - URI path used to construct file path without proper validation
 * @kind path-problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/static-file-handler-path-traversal
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.FlowSources

module StaticFileConfig implements DataFlow::ConfigSig {
  predicate isSource(DataFlow::Node source) {
    source instanceof RemoteFlowSource
  }

  predicate isSink(DataFlow::Node sink) {
    // File/Path construction
    exists(ClassInstanceExpr ce |
      ce.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") and
      sink.asExpr() = ce.getAnArgument()
    )
    or
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("resolve") and
      mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.nio.file", "Path") and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getAnArgument()
    )
    or
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("get") and
      mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.nio.file", "Paths") and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getAnArgument()
    )
  }
  
  predicate isBarrier(DataFlow::Node node) {
    // If toRealPath or getCanonicalPath is called, consider it a barrier
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      (mc.getMethod().hasName("toRealPath") or 
       mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or
       mc.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile")) and
      node.asExpr() = mc
    )
  }
}

module StaticFileFlow = TaintTracking::Global<StaticFileConfig>;
import StaticFileFlow::PathGraph

from StaticFileFlow::PathNode source, StaticFileFlow::PathNode sink
where 
  StaticFileFlow::flowPath(source, sink) and
  (
    sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Static%") or
    sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%File%") or
    sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Resource%") or
    sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Bucket%") or
    sink.getNode().getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Handler%")
  )
select sink.getNode(), source, sink, "Path traversal from HTTP input $@ to file system access", source.getNode(), "user input"
findings.hypothesis: HTTP request URI/path flowing to file system operations in static file handlers, bucket handlers, etc.

message.text: Path traversal from HTTP input [user input](1) to file system access

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Inadequate path validation using startsWith
 * @description String.startsWith() used for path containment check without canonicalization
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/path-startswith-no-canonicalization
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

from MethodCall startsWithCall, MethodCall fileConstructorOrPath
where
  // startsWith check on path
  startsWithCall.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") and
  startsWithCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "String") and
  // Within the same method where file operations occur
  exists(Method m |
    m = startsWithCall.getEnclosingCallable() and
    fileConstructorOrPath.getEnclosingCallable() = m
  ) and
  // File construction nearby
  (
    fileConstructorOrPath.getMethod().hasName("resolve") and
    fileConstructorOrPath.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.nio.file", "Path")
    or
    fileConstructorOrPath.getMethod().hasName("get") and
    fileConstructorOrPath.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.nio.file", "Paths")
  ) and
  // No canonicalization calls nearby (toRealPath, getCanonicalPath, normalize)
  not exists(MethodCall normCall |
    normCall.getEnclosingCallable() = startsWithCall.getEnclosingCallable() and
    (
      normCall.getMethod().hasName("toRealPath") or
      normCall.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or
      normCall.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile")
    )
  )
select startsWithCall, "Path containment check using startsWith() without prior canonicalization - may be bypassable with '../' sequences"
findings.hypothesis: String.startsWith() used for path containment check without canonicalization, allowing bypass via ../ sequences

message.text: Path containment check using startsWith() without prior canonicalization - may be bypassable with '../' sequences
Path containment check using startsWith() without prior canonicalization - may be bypassable with '../' sequences

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name StaticFileHandler - string contains check for dotdot bypass
 * @description StaticFileHandler uses string contains() checks for '..' which is bypassable via URL encoding or unicode
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/static-file-dotdot-string-check
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 *       external/cwe/cwe-706
 */

import java

// Find the sanitizePath method and its conditions - specifically looking for string-level ".." checks
// without prior URL decoding or unicode normalization
from Method sanitizeMethod, MethodCall containsCall
where
  sanitizeMethod.getDeclaringType().getName() = "StaticFileHandler" and
  containsCall.getEnclosingCallable() = sanitizeMethod and
  containsCall.getMethod().hasName("contains") and
  containsCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "String") and
  // No toRealPath or getCanonicalPath in method
  not exists(MethodCall normCall |
    normCall.getEnclosingCallable() = sanitizeMethod and
    (normCall.getMethod().hasName("toRealPath") or
     normCall.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or
     normCall.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile"))
  )
select containsCall, "StaticFileHandler sanitization uses String.contains() check at line " + 
  containsCall.getLocation().getStartLine() + 
  " without prior path canonicalization (toRealPath/getCanonicalPath). This check can be bypassed via URL-encoded sequences (e.g. %2e%2e) or OS-specific path variations."
findings.hypothesis: StaticFileHandler uses String.contains() for '..' detection which can be bypassed with URL encoding or other path obfuscation techniques

message.text: StaticFileHandler sanitization uses String.contains() check at line 277 without prior path canonicalization (toRealPath/getCanonicalPath). This check can be bypassed via URL-encoded sequences (e.g. %2e%2e) or OS-specific path variations.

StaticFileHandler sanitization uses String.contains() check at line 278 without prior path canonicalization (toRealPath/getCanonicalPath). This check can be bypassed via URL-encoded sequences (e.g. %2e%2e) or OS-specific path variations.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name AngularHandler separate path to locateFile  
 * @description AngularHandler has its own path to locateFile at line 96 with different sanitization
 * @kind path-problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/angular-handler-path-traversal
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.FlowSources

module AngularConfig implements DataFlow::ConfigSig {
  predicate isSource(DataFlow::Node source) {
    source instanceof RemoteFlowSource
  }

  predicate isSink(DataFlow::Node sink) {
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("locateFile") and
      mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getName() = "StaticFileHandler" and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getAnArgument()
    )
    or
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("resolve") and
      mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.nio.file", "Path") and
      mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() = "AngularHandler" and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getAnArgument()
    )
  }
}

module AngularFlow = TaintTracking::Global<AngularConfig>;
import AngularFlow::PathGraph

from AngularFlow::PathNode source, AngularFlow::PathNode sink
where AngularFlow::flowPath(source, sink)
select sink.getNode(), source, sink, 
  "HTTP input $@ reaches locateFile/path.resolve in AngularHandler/StaticFileHandler", 
  source.getNode(), "user-controlled input"
findings.hypothesis: AngularHandler may have separate path to locateFile that bypasses sanitizePath in StaticFileHandler

message.text: HTTP input [user-controlled input](1) reaches locateFile/path.resolve in AngularHandler/StaticFileHandler
HTTP input [user-controlled input](1) reaches locateFile/path.resolve in AngularHandler/StaticFileHandler

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name StaticFileHandler sanitizePath uses String file separator concatenation  
 * @description sanitizePath builds contains() check with File.separator - on Windows uses backslash, on Unix forward slash
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/sanitizepath-separator-concat
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

// Find what the contains() checks at lines 277-278 are actually checking
// The "contains(... + ...)" suggests building pattern with File.separator
from MethodCall mc, BinaryExpr concatExpr
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() = "StaticFileHandler" and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() = "sanitizePath" and
  mc.getMethod().hasName("contains") and
  concatExpr = mc.getArgument(0) and
  (concatExpr instanceof AddExpr)
select mc, "sanitizePath.contains() at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine() + 
  " checks: [" + mc.getArgument(0).toString() + "] - built with concatenation including separator"
findings.hypothesis: sanitizePath builds string patterns using File.separator concatenation - examining what exact patterns are checked

message.text: sanitizePath.contains() at line 277 checks: \[... + ...\] - built with concatenation including separator
sanitizePath.contains() at line 278 checks: \[... + ...\] - built with concatenation including separator
